spatial_scope: Original transit strip and beam geometry
notes: N=89 null visit-days after excluding the discovery from 90 useful visits. One independent scintillation state per visit-day is conservative. The 5-sigma threshold is a configurable model assumption.

spatial_scope: Two nominal Wow positions; small primary beam
notes: Eight hours are relevant for whichever of the two possible positions is correct. Threshold is represented conservatively as 0.2 times the original OSU flux-equivalent, based on the paper's degraded-sensitivity comparison. Not applicable to a kHz-wide line.

linewidth_scope: Comparable to a <10 kHz Wow-like line
notes: Uses the conservative 5.5 mJy rms at the nominal central field and an 8-sigma feature threshold. Relevant dwell is 20 minutes at the true one of two nominal positions.

linewidth_scope: Comparable to a <10 kHz Wow-like line
notes: Uses the conservative 3.7 mJy rms central-field value and an 8-sigma threshold. Each nominal position was observed for 43 minutes.

spatial_scope: Six pointings/runs covering nominal positions and declination offsets
notes: Six nearly 14-hour runs. One independent state per run is deliberately conservative. Published all-data threshold was about 18 Jy.

confidence: High for totals; conservative state count
notes: More than 100 hours were obtained in sessions no longer than three hours, so at least 34 separate sessions are required. The 1-minute empirical rms was 1.247 Jy and the all-search statistical threshold was 8 sigma. One state per session is conservative.

Two 30-minute GBT observations and six 5-minute ATA observations, with 580 seconds of simultaneous overlap. Excluded from the core likelihood because the published summary used here does not provide a directly comparable Jy threshold and the search covered only one proposed star.
